def solve(s)
    VOWELS = ['a', 'e', 'i', 'o', 'u']

    # 1. Collect data: counts and first indices for each unique vowel present
    counts = Hash.new(0)
    first_index = {}
    
    s.each_char.with_index do |char, i|
        if VOWELS.include?(char)
            counts[char] += 1
            if !first_index.key?(char)
                first_index[char] = i
            end
        end
    end

    # 2. Get unique vowels present and sort them
    unique_vowels = counts.keys
    
    # Sort criteria:
    # 1. Frequency descending (count2 <=> count1)
    # 2. First Appearance ascending (first_index[v1] <=> first_index[v2])
    sorted_vowels = unique_vowels.sort do |v1, v2|
        count1 = counts[v1]
        count2 = counts[v2]
        
        # Primary sort: Frequency descending
        if count1 != count2
            count2 <=> count1
        else
            # Secondary sort: First appearance ascending
            first_index[v1] <=> first_index[v2]
        end
    end

    # 3. Build the complete sorted vowel sequence (V_sorted)
    v_sorted_list = []
    sorted_vowels.each do |v|
        # Append vowel v, counts[v] times
        v_sorted_list.concat([v] * counts[v])
    end
    
    # 4. Reconstruct the string by replacing original vowels with sorted sequence
    result = []
    v_ptr = 0
    
    s.each_char do |char|
        if VOWELS.include?(char)
            # Replace vowel with the next sorted vowel
            result << v_sorted_list[v_ptr]
            v_ptr += 1
        else
            # Keep consonants unchanged
            result << char
        end
    end
    
    return result.join
end

def solve(s)
    vowels = ['a', 'e', 'i', 'o', 'u']
    
    # 1. Analysis (Frequency, First Occurrence, Vowel Positions)
    counts = Hash.new(0)
    first_occurrence = {}
    vowel_indices = []
    
    for i, char in s.each_with_index.to_a
        if vowels.include?(char)
            # Count frequency
            counts[char] += 1
            
            # Record first occurrence
            unless first_occurrence.key?(char)
                first_occurrence[char] = i
            end
            
            # Record all vowel positions
            vowel_indices << i
        end
    end
    
    # 2. Prepare unique vowels for sorting
    unique_vowels = vowels.select { |v| counts.key?(v) }
    
    # 3. Sorting Unique Vowels
    # Sort criteria: 
    # 1. Frequency (Descending) -> -counts[v]
    # 2. First Appearance (Ascending) -> first_occurrence[v]
    
    sorted_unique_vowels = unique_vowels.sort do |v1, v2|
        freq1 = counts[v1]
        freq2 = counts[v2]
        
        # Primary sort: Frequency descending
        if freq1 != freq2
            freq2 <=> freq1 # Descending order (b <=> a)
        else
            # Secondary sort: First appearance ascending
            first_occurrence[v1] <=> first_occurrence[v2]
        end
    end
    
    # 4. Construct Sorted Vowel Sequence
    sorted_vowel_sequence = []
    for v in sorted_unique_vowels
        count = counts[v]
        (1..count).each do
            sorted_vowel_sequence << v
        end
    end
    
    # 5. Reconstruction
    s_chars = s.chars
    
    # Place the sorted vowels back into the original vowel positions
    for k in 0...(vowel_indices.length)
        index_to_replace = vowel_indices[k]
        new_vowel = sorted_vowel_sequence[k]
        s_chars[index_to_replace] = new_vowel
    end
    
    # 6. Return result
    s_chars.join
end
